# docuchat

> docuchat is a private, local AI for legal documents. It reads your contracts, transcripts, pleadings, and exhibits and answers plain-English questions with every fact pinned to the exact page and span it came from. It runs 100% on your own computer: no cloud, no account, no telemetry. Free and open source (MIT).

Key facts: inference, embeddings, OCR, and storage are all local (binds to loopback `127.0.0.1` only). Every cited quote is mechanically verified character-by-character against the source; a fabricated or altered quote yields zero citations. On our open evaluation, page+span citation accuracy was 98.4% (62 of 63) with 0 displayed fabrications. It is not an AI lawyer and gives no legal advice: it locates and summarizes what your documents say, with citations you verify.

## Docs
- [Website](https://docuchat.app/): product overview, setup, and download
- [README](https://github.com/janderswag/docuchat.app/blob/main/README.md): features, cloud-AI comparison, FAQ, architecture, tech stack
- [Architecture decisions](https://github.com/janderswag/docuchat.app/blob/main/DECISIONS.md): why hand-rolled RAG, why LanceDB, why mechanical citation verification

## About
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/janderswag/docuchat.app): MIT-licensed source
- [Get docuchat](https://github.com/janderswag/docuchat.app): run from source today; signed macOS and Windows apps coming soon
